patches.hypothesis: The original patch overfits by handling only the single-element case separately. To address this, we need to ensure that the method handles all edge cases, including empty arrays, and maintains the intended algorithm for accurate computation.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug occurs because the code attempts to access the second element of the prodHigh array when the input arrays have only one element, leading to an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

patches.hypothesis: The refined fix addresses overfitting by adding checks for edge cases such as empty arrays, arrays with all zero elements, and null inputs. This ensures the method handles a broader range of scenarios gracefully.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug occurs when the input arrays have only one element. The code attempts to access prodHigh[1] without checking if the length of the array is greater than 1, leading to an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

patches.hypothesis: The original patch addresses the specific issue of single-element arrays but does not handle empty arrays or very large arrays. The refined fix will add checks for empty arrays and ensure the method can handle edge cases gracefully without introducing performance issues.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug occurs when the input arrays have a single element, causing an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException when trying to access prodHigh[1]. The fix involves handling the case where the length of the array is 1 separately to avoid accessing out-of-bounds indices.
